description: A Solar Design Engineer needs to evaluate the self-consumption and solar fraction of a proposed 100 kW DC commercial PV system in Phoenix, AZ under a Net Energy Metering (NEM) policy. Model the system using PySAM (Pvwattsv8) with the weather data at /home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/phoenix_az_tmy.csv and the following specifications: 100.0 kW DC capacity, Standard module type (0), Fixed - Roof Mount array (1), 10 degree tilt, 180 degree azimuth, 14.0% system losses, 1.15 DC-to-AC ratio, and 96% inverter efficiency. Compare the simulated hourly AC generation against the hourly commercial load profile found at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/commercial_load_8760.csv to determine the annual energy balance, self-consumption ratio (%), and solar fraction (%). Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/self_consumption_results.json using the following numeric keys: "annual_energy_kwh", "annual_load_kwh", "annual_energy_exported_kwh", "annual_energy_imported_kwh", "self_consumption_ratio_pct", and "solar_fraction_pct".
